metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_5299
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 4a: SynCAM 2 (CADM2) in enriched in synaptic plasma membrane biochemical fractions prepared from rat forebrain at P9 (as compared to e.g. synaptosomes).

Figure 4b: confocal imaging of immunofluorescence labeling for SynCAM 2 (CADM2), GAD65 (GAD2) and PSD-95 (DLG4) in cultured hippocampal neurons from showed SynCAM 2 is localized to both inhibory and excitatory synapses.

Figure 8: SynCAM 1 and 2 coimmunoprecipitate in synaptosomes prepared from rat forebrain at P15.

Figure 9: in mixed coculture assay of HEK 293 cells expressing SynCAM 1 or 2 seeded atop dissociated hippocampal cultures, SynCAM 1 and 2 recruit presynaptic marker proteins and engage each other in adhesive neuronal complexes.

Figure 10: Postsynaptic overexpression of SynCAM 2 promotes formation of presynaptic terminals in dissociated hippocampal neurons and promotes excitatory synaptic transmission.. Experimental description: most assays used brain tissue and cultures from rats:
"For affinity chromatography on SynCAM extracellular domains, rat forebrain homogenate was prepared in homogenization buffer"
"Electrophysiological analysis was performed in primary hippocampal cultures prepared from embryonic day 18 (E18) to E19 Sprague Dawley rats."
"The indicated subcellular fractions were prepared from rat forebrain at P9."

Overexpressed proteins were mouse species:
"Sequences encoding full-length SynCAM 2 (splice product 2) (Biederer, 2006), SynCAM 3 (splice product 2), and SynCAM 4 were amplified from a mouse brain cDNA library prepared at 9-11 weeks of age (Clontech #639400)."

antibody:
"Antibodies against SynCAM 2 (YU524) and SynCAM 3 (YU525) were raised in rabbits against the peptides CKDVKYLKEEDANRKT and CHGDQTRIQEDPNGKT, respectively, corresponding to amino acid sequences in the second Ig-like domain of the mouse proteins with an N-terminal cysteine for coupling."
